page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9T24 processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing it in the top 25% of its class. Multi-core performance, as measured by stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the average range. However, memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck; both small-block cached and large-block uncached read-only memory bandwidth benchmarks register in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET performance are relatively weak, though single-threaded compression and decompression tasks maintain average throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c8a.xlarge offers a dedicated compute profile without the added expense of bundled local storage or high memory allocations. This makes it a viable option for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or high-speed local disk access. Recommended workloads include single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and compute-intensive applications that can operate within the 8.0 GB memory limit and rely on network-attached storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this KVM-based server does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing it in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, while multi-core performance and cryptographic execution remain average. However, memory read bandwidth and database operations are weaker, positioning this instance as a specialized choice for compute-heavy, memory-light tasks. It is best suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, and distributed compute workloads that utilize network-attached storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, offering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.xlarge is a KVM-based compute-optimized server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated four-core AMD EPYC compute instance with strong single-core performance and a lean memory profile for compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9T24 processor with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs
8.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no bundled local storage
Well-suited for single-threaded processing and lightweight compute tasks
